question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/M3 EA Architecture, (2) patterns/Tool Surface as Audit Surface, (3) questions/Where is the Autonomy Line, (4) articles/AI Landscape 2026, (5) numbers/M3 Eval Lab 0 Runs, (6) patterns/SkillOpt Pipeline.

connections.bridge: Where is the Autonomy Line, M3 Eval Lab 0 Runs, and SkillOpt Pipeline together describe a discipline gap: every design needs a measurement, and the measurement is not yet running. The pattern is design-without-measure, and the cost is invisible until the design fails.

Tool Surface as Audit Surface and AI Landscape 2026 (consolidation) are the same move at different layers — fewer, sharper, more-auditable tools wins over broad, easy-to-call APIs, both in agent design and in market structure.
M3 EA Architecture says the operator can do more; M3 Eval Lab 0 Runs says we have not measured what happens when the operator does more. The tension is the Step 2 conversation.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/State of Mavis, (2) patterns/Session Continuity, (3) articles/Operation Apex Notes, (4) questions/When to Update State of Mavis, (5) patterns/Friction Log Discipline, (6) ideas/End of Session Routine.

connections.bridge: State of Mavis, Session Continuity, and End of Session Routine together define a new artifact: a per-session MOC that captures where-the-work-is, distinct from the daily note (one day) and MAVIS.md (one week). The pattern is multi-scale memory.

Friction Log Discipline and Operation Apex Notes are the same reflexion loop at different cadences — Friction Log is per-session, Operation Apex was per-project. The pattern is write-it-down-when-stuck, and the value compounds.

State of Mavis is the artifact, End of Session Routine is the discipline. Without the routine, the artifact goes stale; without the artifact, the routine has nothing to write. The tension is operational, not conceptual.
